items.title: Winter 2025 SOC 1 report is now available with 184 services in scope
items.snippet: Amazon Web Services (AWS) is pleased to announce that the Winter 2025 System and Organization Controls (SOC) 1 report is now available. The report covers 184 services over the 12-month period from January 1, 2025 – December 31, 2025, giving customers a full…

items.title: Amazon SageMaker now supports multi-region replication from IAM Identity Center
items.snippet: Amazon SageMaker now supports multi-region replication from IAM Identity Center (IdC), enabling you to deploy SageMaker Unified Studio domains in different regions from your IdC instance. This new capability empowers enterprise customers, particularly those…

items.title: Five new Qwen models for coding agents and efficient reasoning are now available in Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
items.snippet: Today, AWS announced the availability of Qwen3-Coder-Next, Qwen3-30B-A3B, Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking-2507, Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct, and Qwen3.5-4B in Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, expanding the portfolio of foundation models available to AWS customers. These…

items.title: Introducing the Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes gateway for hybrid Kubernetes networking
items.snippet: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) now offers the Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes gateway, a feature that automates networking between your Amazon EKS cluster VPC and Kubernetes Pods running on Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes. The Amazon EKS Hybrid Nodes gateway…

items.title: AWS Marketplace streamlines VAT payment for deemed supply transactions
items.snippet: AWS Marketplace now offers sellers a streamlined self-service process to submit Value Added Tax (VAT) invoices and receive automated VAT disbursements for deemed supply of digital services in the European Union, Norway, and the United Kingdom. Under the…

items.title: Amazon Athena Spark adds support for AWS PrivateLink
items.snippet: Amazon Athena Spark now supports AWS PrivateLink so that you can access APIs and endpoints from your Amazon Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) without traversing the public internet. This feature can help you meet compliance requirements by allowing you to access…

items.title: AWS Lambda functions can now mount Amazon S3 buckets as file systems with S3 Files
items.snippet: AWS Lambda now supports Amazon S3 Files, enabling your Lambda functions to mount Amazon S3 buckets as file systems and perform standard file operations without downloading data for processing. Built using Amazon EFS, S3 Files gives you the performance and…

items.title: Real-time analytics: Oldcastle integrates Infor with Amazon Aurora and Amazon Quick Sight
items.snippet: This post explores how Oldcastle used AWS services to transform their analytics and AI capabilities by integrating Infor ERP with Amazon Aurora and Amazon Quick Sight. We discuss how they overcame the limitations of traditional cloud ERP reporting to deploy…

items.title: Amazon CloudWatch pipelines now supports configuration of processors via AI
items.snippet: Amazon CloudWatch pipelines now lets you configure log processors using natural language descriptions powered by generative AI. CloudWatch pipelines is a fully managed service that ingests, transforms, and routes log data to CloudWatch without requiring you…

items.title: AWS Glue now supports OAuth 2.0 for Snowflake connectivity
items.snippet: Starting today, AWS Glue supports OAuth 2.0 authorization and authentication for native Snowflake connectivity, enabling customers to read from and write to Snowflake without sharing user credentials. This makes it easier for enterprises to maintain security…

items.title: AWS Transform custom is now available in six additional AWS Regions
items.snippet: AWS Transform custom is now available in six additional AWS Regions: Asia Pacific (Mumbai, Tokyo, Seoul, Sydney), Canada (Central), and Europe (London). AWS Transform custom enables organizations to modernize and transform code at scale using AWS-managed and…

items.title: Amazon EC2 G7e instances now available in AWS Local Zones in Los Angeles
items.snippet: Today, AWS announces the general availability of Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (Amazon EC2) G7e instances in AWS Local Zones in Los Angeles, California. G7e instances feature NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs and 5th generation Intel Xeon…

items.title: Amazon Location Service now offers bulk address validation for the United States, Canada, Australia, and the United Kingdom
items.snippet: Amazon Location Service now offers bulk address validation for the United States, Canada, Australia, and the United Kingdom. Customers can now validate, correct, and standardize large volumes of addresses at scale, whether cleaning customer databases before a…

items.title: Amazon Aurora serverless: Up to 30% better performance, smarter scaling, and still scales to zero
items.snippet: Amazon Aurora serverless — the autoscaling database that scales up to support your most demanding workloads and down to zero when you don't need it — just got faster and smarter, with up to 30% better performance than the previous version and enhanced scaling…

items.title: AWS Backup adds Amazon Redshift Serverless and Aurora DSQL support for AWS Organizations backup policies
items.snippet: AWS Backup now supports Amazon Redshift Serverless namespaces and Amazon Aurora DSQL clusters as resource types in AWS Organizations backup policies. Organization administrators can now define backup policy rules that directly target these resource types…

items.title: AWS Lambda Durable Execution SDK for Java GA
items.snippet: Today, AWS announces the general availability of the AWS Lambda Durable Execution SDK for Java, empowering Java developers to build resilient, long-running workflows using Lambda durable functions. With this SDK, developers can create multi-step applications…

items.title: Amazon Connect Outbound Campaigns now supports contact priority ordering
items.snippet: Amazon Connect Outbound Campaigns now allows you to dial contacts in configurable priority order based on up to 10 profile attributes for voice campaigns and voice activities in journeys. This helps you focus agent time on the most valuable customers or…

items.title: Amazon EBS expands volume modification enhancement to AWS European Sovereign Cloud Region
items.snippet: Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) now supports up to four Elastic Volumes modifications per volume within a rolling 24-hour window in AWS European Sovereign Cloud (Germany) Region. Elastic Volumes modifications allow you to increase the size, change the…

items.title: Amazon EVS now offers Microsoft Windows Server Licensing
items.snippet: Today, we're announcing that Amazon Elastic VMware Service (Amazon EVS) now offers Microsoft Windows Server licensing entitlements. You can now migrate or create new virtual machines (VMs) running Windows Server OS in EVS and obtain Windows Server licensing…

items.title: Amazon Connect expands agentic voice speech-to-speech experiences to three new AWS Regions and ten locales
items.snippet: Amazon Connect now expands agentic voice speech-to-speech experiences to three additional AWS Regions: Asia Pacific (Seoul), Asia Pacific (Singapore), and Europe (Frankfurt), along with new locales including Australian English, British English, Singaporean…

items.title: AWS IoT Greengrass v2.17 now supports non-root installation and introduces new light weight components
items.snippet: AWS IoT Greengrass v2.17 is now available, enabling you to run the edge runtime as a non-root user on Linux systems and deploy lighter-weight components that use significantly less memory. AWS IoT Greengrass is an Internet of Things (IoT) edge runtime and…

items.title: Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility) now supports in-place upgrade from version 5.0 to 8.0
items.snippet: Amazon DocumentDB (with MongoDB compatibility) supports in-place major version upgrade (MVU) from version 5.0 to 8.0. You can upgrade with just a few clicks in the AWS Management Console or via the AWS SDK or AWS CLI — no new clusters, no endpoint changes,…

items.title: Amazon EKS enhances cluster governance with new IAM condition keys
items.snippet: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) now supports seven additional IAM condition keys for cluster creation and configuration APIs, enhancing the governance controls available through IAM policies and Service Control Policies (SCPs). Organizations managing…

items.title: Amazon Connect outbound campaigns now supports hourly segment refresh
items.snippet: Amazon Connect Outbound Campaigns now allows you to refresh campaign segments as frequently as every hour, reduced from the previous minimum of 24 hours. This enables campaigns to reach newly eligible customers throughout the day rather than waiting for the…

items.title: Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights introduces JOIN and sub-query commands
items.snippet: Amazon CloudWatch Logs Insights introduces JOIN and sub-query commands to the Logs Insights query language to accelerate log analysis. Customers who need to analyze logs across multiple log groups or correlate data from different sources no longer need to run…

items.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Claude Opus 4.7 in Amazon Bedrock, AWS Interconnect GA, and more (April 20, 2026)
items.snippet: Claude Opus 4.7 arrives in Amazon Bedrock with improved agentic coding and a 1M token context window. AWS Interconnect reaches general availability with multicloud private connectivity and a new last-mile option. Plus, post-quantum TLS for Secrets Manager,…

items.title: Amazon MSK Replicator now supports log forwarding for replication visibility
items.snippet: Amazon MSK Replicator now delivers replicator logs to give you end-to-end visibility into replication health. Replicator logs surface critical replication events and errors along with guidance on how to resolve each issue, enabling you to troubleshoot faster…

items.title: Amazon MSK Replicator now supports enhanced consumer offset synchronization for bidirectional replication
items.snippet: Amazon MSK Replicator now provides enhanced consumer offset synchronization for bidirectional replication, enabling applications to resume processing from the correct position when moving across Kafka clusters. This capability enables you to move producer and…

items.title: MSK Replicator now supports replication from external Apache Kafka clusters to MSK Express Brokers
items.snippet: Amazon MSK Replicator now supports data replication from external Apache Kafka clusters—including on-premises, self-managed on AWS, or other cloud providers—to Amazon MSK Express brokers. This capability simplifies workload migration to MSK Express Brokers,…
